data.content: The bug is clear — `ProcessPayment` only validates zero, not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test`, which auto-reruns tests on every file save. Let me set that up and apply the fix:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to add `amount < 0` (or `amount <= 0`) to the validation.

The user wants the fastest workflow. I should:
1. Start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so it auto-reruns on file changes
2. Then fix the bug
3. The test will auto-rerun without manual rebuild

Let me do this.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-69bb61011a9243f5a38da3197d9c8e5b && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test
